title: Webinar: How to Automate Exposure Validation to Match the Speed of AI Attacks
summary: In February 2026, researchers uncovered a shift that completely changed the game: threat actors are now using custom AI setups to automate attacks directly into the kill chain.
card_summary: In February 2026, researchers uncovered a shift that completely changed the game: threat actors are now using custom AI setups to automate attacks directly into the kill chain.

sections.impact:
We’re talking about autonomous agents mapping Active Directory and seizing Domain Admin credentials in minutes.

Assessed severity: MEDIUM.
sections.context: This reflects continued use of phishing as an initial-access path in broader intrusion chains.

email_summary: In February 2026, researchers uncovered a shift that completely changed the game: threat actors are now using custom AI setups to automate attacks directly into the kill chain. We aren't just talking about AI writing better phishing emails anymore.

title: What to Look for in an Exposure Management Platform (And What Most of Them Get Wrong)
summary: Every security team has a version of the same story. The quarter ends with hundreds of vulnerabilities closed. The dashboards are bursting with green. Then someone in a leadership meeting asks: "So, are we actually safer now?"
card_summary: Every security team has a version of the same story. The quarter ends with hundreds of vulnerabilities closed.

sections.impact:
The quarter ends with hundreds of vulnerabilities closed.

Assessed severity: MEDIUM.
sections.mitigation: The room goes quiet because an honest answer requires context – which is something that patch counts and CVSS scores were never designed to provide.

email_summary: Every security team has a version of the same story. The quarter ends with hundreds of vulnerabilities closed.

title: Critical cPanel Authentication Vulnerability Identified — Update Your Server Immediately
summary: cPanel has released security updates to address a security issue impacting various authentication paths that could allow an attacker to obtain access to the control panel software.
card_summary: cPanel has released security updates to address a security issue impacting various authentication paths that could allow an attacker to obtain access to the control panel software.
email_summary: cPanel has released security updates to address a security issue impacting various authentication paths that could allow an attacker to obtain access to the control panel software. The problem affects all currently supported versions, according to an alert released by cPanel on Tuesday.

title: CISA Adds Actively Exploited ConnectWise and Windows Flaws to KEV
summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday added two security flaws impacting ConnectWise ScreenConnect and Microsoft Windows to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, based on evidence of active exploitation.
card_summary: Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday added two security flaws impacting ConnectWise ScreenConnect and Microsoft Windows to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, based on evidence of active exploitation.
quick_take: Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday added two security flaws impacting ConnectWise ScreenConnect and Microsoft Windows to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, based on…

sections.technical:
The vulnerabilities are listed below -

CVE-2024-1708 (CVSS score: 8.4) - A path traversal vulnerability in ConnectWise ScreenConnect Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday added two security flaws impacting ConnectWise ScreenConnect and Microsoft Windows to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, based on evidence of active exploitation.

Referenced vulnerabilities: CVE-2024-1708.

email_summary: Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday added two security flaws impacting ConnectWise ScreenConnect and Microsoft Windows to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, based on evidence of active exploitation. Tracked as CVE-2024-1708. Severity is rated high.

title: Lotus Wiper Attack Targets Venezuelan Energy Firms, Utilities
summary: An analysis of the destructive malware reveals sophisticated living-off-the-land (LotL) techniques and detailed strategies for the widespread deletion of data.
card_summary: An analysis of the destructive malware reveals sophisticated living-off-the-land (LotL) techniques and detailed strategies for the widespread deletion of data.

title: LiteLLM CVE-2026-42208 SQL Injection Exploited within 36 Hours of Disclosure
summary: In yet another instance of threat actors quickly jumping on the exploitation bandwagon, a newly disclosed critical security flaw in BerriAI's LiteLLM Python package has come under active exploitation in the wild within 36 hours of the bug becoming public knowledge.
card_summary: In yet another instance of threat actors quickly jumping on the exploitation bandwagon, a newly disclosed critical security flaw in BerriAI's LiteLLM Python package has come under active exploitation in the wild within 36 hours of the bug becoming public…

title: Learning from the Vercel breach: Shadow AI & OAuth sprawl
summary: A single third-party OAuth integration can become a direct path into your environment. Push explains how the Vercel breach shows a compromised OAuth app can lead to widespread impact across downstream customers. [...]

title: GitHub fixes RCE flaw that gave access to millions of private repos

title: CISA orders feds to patch Windows flaw exploited as zero-day
summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has ordered federal agencies to secure their Windows systems against a vulnerability exploited in zero-day attacks. [...]
card_summary: Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has ordered federal agencies to secure their Windows systems against a vulnerability exploited in zero-day attacks. [...]

sections.context: This story is part of the recurring zero-day pattern where exploitation pressure is highest before broad patch adoption.
